failure_modes.text: Anchoring and framing can corrupt first principles work before the reasoning even starts by locking attention onto the wrong premise.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the starting premises: anchoring and framing can corrupt first-principles work before the reasoning even begins.

failure_modes.text: Confirmation bias and groupthink can make teams defend the first chosen principles instead of testing them against opposing evidence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require the principles to survive opposition: confirmation bias and groupthink will defend the first chosen premises unless the antithesis is actively tested.

failure_modes.text: Analysis paralysis can turn first principles into endless premise polishing rather than actionable understanding.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a deadline for moving from premises to actionable understanding; endless premise polishing is analysis paralysis wearing first-principles clothing.

premortem_questions.text: What would we have to believe to accept the opposite thesis?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What would we have to believe to accept the opposite thesis?"

premortem_questions.text: What is the single core point we want the audience to remember?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What is the single core point we want the audience to remember?"

premortem_questions.text: Are we unknowingly influenced by our perspective and have we actively tried different frames?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "Are we unknowingly influenced by our perspective (frame of reference), and have we actively tried to look from a variety of different frames?"

heuristics.text: Use repeated 'why' questions to strip the problem down to its elemental truths.
Disaggregate the problem into logical parts and stress-test the first hypothesis instead of reasoning from unexamined wholes.
Explain the rebuilt logic in plain English to reveal any blind spots that still hide behind abstraction.
heuristics.source_quote: Write down an explanation of the concept in plain English, simple enough for a five-year-old to understand, revealing gaps in mastery.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on premise stripping, opposing-thesis challenge, and anti-abstraction clarity checks, so the chunk set stays near those interventions.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor innovation mythology because the raw file is more useful as a disciplined assumption-breaking and simplification method.
open_questions: The third premortem question is slightly compacted from a longer raw prompt so it remains chunk-usable.

allies: The raw allies section is rich on simplicity, hypothesis-driven problem solving, and latticework expansion for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strong on System 1 shortcuts, curse-of-knowledge, and motivated reasoning.
structured_tensions: There is no explicit structured tension block, so any later tension preservation should remain conservative and review-visible.
